# Functional Requirements Quality Checklist: SPEC-008 Perspectives

**Purpose**: Validate that all 5 user stories have complete, clear, and consistent functional requirements; legacy migration is fully specified; edge cases are covered; and UI side-effect warnings are properly documented.
**Created**: 2026-03-18
**Feature**: specs/008-perspectives (spec.md, plan.md, data-model.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness -- User Story 1 (List All Perspectives) to FR Mapping

- [ ] CHK001 - Are functional requirements FR-001 through FR-008 traceable to all 5 acceptance scenarios in User Story 1? [Completeness, Spec US1]
- [ ] CHK002 - Is the acceptance scenario for "no custom perspectives" (US1-AS5) mapped to a functional requirement specifying that an empty array is returned (not an error)? [Completeness, Spec US1-AS5, FR-002]
- [x] CHK003 - Does FR-006 (alphabetical sorting within type groups) have a corresponding acceptance scenario, or is it only specified in requirements without a testable scenario? [Gap, Spec FR-006] -- RESOLVED: Added US1-AS6 acceptance scenario covering alphabetical sorting within type groups and totalCount validation.
- [x] CHK004 - Does FR-007 (totalCount field) have a corresponding acceptance scenario, or is it only specified in requirements without a testable scenario? [Gap, Spec FR-007] -- RESOLVED: Added US1-AS6 acceptance scenario: totalCount must equal the length of the returned perspectives array.
- [ ] CHK005 - Are requirements specified for what `list_perspectives` returns when no parameters are provided (US1-AS1), including both the default `type: "all"` behavior and the response structure? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, US1-AS1]

## Requirement Completeness -- User Story 2 (Get Perspective Details) to FR Mapping

- [ ] CHK006 - Are functional requirements FR-009 through FR-016 traceable to all 5 acceptance scenarios in User Story 2? [Completeness, Spec US2]
- [ ] CHK007 - Is the acceptance scenario for "perspective name does not exist" (US2-AS4) mapped to FR-013 (error on not found), and does FR-013 specify the expected error code? [Completeness, Spec FR-013, US2-AS4]
- [ ] CHK008 - Does the spec define what "limited configurable properties" means for built-in perspectives (US2-AS2, FR-016), or is this phrase ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK009 - Is the disambiguation scenario (FR-014) covered by an acceptance scenario in User Story 2, or is disambiguation only specified in requirements? [Gap, Spec FR-014] -- RESOLVED: Added US2-AS6 acceptance scenario: multiple custom perspectives sharing same name triggers disambiguation error with DISAMBIGUATION_REQUIRED code and candidates array.
- [x] CHK010 - Does FR-012a (version-gated filter rules at v4.2+) have a corresponding acceptance scenario showing what happens when OmniFocus is below v4.2? [Gap, Spec FR-012a] -- RESOLVED: Added US1-AS7 and US2-AS7 acceptance scenarios: below v4.2, filterAggregation and filterRules are returned as null (graceful degradation, not error).

## Requirement Completeness -- User Story 3 (Switch Active Perspective) to FR Mapping

- [ ] CHK011 - Are functional requirements FR-017 through FR-024 traceable to all 6 acceptance scenarios in User Story 3? [Completeness, Spec US3]
- [ ] CHK012 - Does FR-022 (UI side-effect warning in tool description) specify the exact wording or minimum content that the warning must include? [Clarity, Spec FR-022]
- [ ] CHK013 - Is FR-022 clear about WHERE the warning appears -- in the MCP tool's `description` field, in the response, or both? [Clarity, Spec FR-022]
- [ ] CHK014 - Does the spec define what "confirmation of the switch" (US3-AS1, FR-021) includes -- just the perspective name, or also the previous perspective? [Clarity, Spec FR-021]
- [ ] CHK015 - Is the idempotency behavior (US3-AS5, FR-023) specified clearly -- does the response differ between "actually switched" and "already active" (e.g., a no-op code like ALREADY_ACTIVE)? [Clarity, Spec FR-023]
- [ ] CHK016 - Does FR-020 specify the exact error code for the no-window case (e.g., `NO_WINDOW`), or just "an error"? [Completeness, Spec FR-020]

## Requirement Completeness -- User Story 4 (Export Perspective) to FR Mapping

- [ ] CHK017 - Are functional requirements FR-025 through FR-032 traceable to all 5 acceptance scenarios in User Story 4? [Completeness, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK018 - Does FR-026 (error on built-in export) specify the exact error code (e.g., `BUILTIN_NOT_EXPORTABLE`)? [Completeness, Spec FR-026]
- [ ] CHK019 - Is the behavior for FR-030 (no `saveTo`, return configuration data) sufficiently specified -- does the spec define what "base64-encoded if binary" means and when it applies? [Clarity, Spec FR-030]
- [ ] CHK020 - Does FR-032 (invalid directory error) specify the error code (e.g., `INVALID_DIRECTORY`) and distinguish between "does not exist" and "not writable"? [Clarity, Spec FR-032]

## Requirement Completeness -- User Story 5 (Set Perspective Icon Color) to FR Mapping

- [ ] CHK021 - Are functional requirements FR-033 through FR-039 traceable to all 4 acceptance scenarios in User Story 5? [Completeness, Spec US5]
- [ ] CHK022 - Does FR-034 (error on built-in modification) specify the exact error code (e.g., `BUILTIN_NOT_MODIFIABLE`)? [Completeness, Spec FR-034]
- [ ] CHK023 - Is the color validation requirement (FR-036) complete -- does it specify what error message is returned for invalid hex formats? [Completeness, Spec FR-036, US5-AS3]
- [x] CHK024 - Does the spec define what happens when a valid color is set on a perspective that already has an icon color (overwrite vs error)? [Gap, Spec FR-035] -- RESOLVED: Added edge case to spec: overwrite is correct idempotent behavior. Setting iconColor on a perspective that already has one simply overwrites the previous value.

## Requirement Completeness -- Legacy Migration

- [ ] CHK025 - Does the spec enumerate ALL legacy files to be deleted, and is this list consistent between the spec (Migration Context section) and plan (Files to Delete)? [Completeness, Spec Migration Context]
- [ ] CHK026 - Is the removal of the `OmnifocusPerspective` interface from `src/types.ts` specified with sufficient detail -- remove the interface only, or remove the entire file? [Clarity, Spec Migration Context]
- [ ] CHK027 - Does FR-008 (replace legacy `list_perspectives`) clearly state that the NEW tool uses the SAME tool name as the legacy tool? [Clarity, Spec FR-008]
- [ ] CHK028 - Does FR-015 (replace legacy `get_perspective_view`) clearly state that the old tool NAME is retired (not just replaced) and the new tool uses a DIFFERENT name (`get_perspective`)? [Clarity, Spec FR-015]
- [x] CHK029 - Are requirements specified for what happens to MCP clients that still reference the retired `get_perspective_view` tool name after migration? [Gap, Spec FR-015] -- RESOLVED: Added to spec Migration Context: clients referencing retired tool name get standard "tool not found" error from MCP server. Clean break, no compatibility shim or redirect.
- [ ] CHK030 - Is the removal of `PerspectiveItem` interface documented alongside `OmnifocusPerspective`, or is it missing from the cleanup list? [Completeness, Spec Migration Context]
- [x] CHK031 - Does the spec define the order of migration steps (delete old files first, then create new, or vice versa) to avoid build breakage? [Gap, Spec Migration Context] -- RESOLVED: Added to spec Migration Context and plan AD-001: (1) create new files first, (2) delete legacy files, (3) update server.ts. Ensures build never references deleted files.

## Requirement Consistency -- Cross-Requirement Alignment

- [ ] CHK032 - Is the `type` filter parameter in FR-003 consistent with the `PerspectiveTypeSchema` in the contracts (`z.enum(['builtin', 'custom'])` + `"all"`)? [Consistency, Spec FR-003 vs perspective-identifier.ts]
- [ ] CHK033 - Does FR-004 (built-in list: Inbox, Projects, Tags, Forecast, Flagged, Review) match the `BUILT_IN_PERSPECTIVE_NAMES` const in the contracts? [Consistency, Spec FR-004 vs contracts]
- [ ] CHK034 - Is the lookup precedence rule (identifier over name) stated consistently across FR-009, FR-017, FR-025, FR-033, and the data-model Lookup Behavior table? [Consistency, Spec Lookup Behavior]
- [ ] CHK035 - Does the error handling section (FR-040 through FR-043) apply equally to all 5 tools, and is this cross-reference explicit? [Consistency, Spec FR-040]

## Scenario Coverage -- Edge Cases

- [ ] CHK036 - Is the "no custom perspectives" edge case specified with requirements for ALL tools that query custom perspectives (list, get, export, set_icon), not just `list_perspectives`? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK037 - Is the "built-in perspective export attempt" edge case (FR-026) covered with a specific acceptance scenario in User Story 4? [Coverage, Spec US4-AS2]
- [ ] CHK038 - Is the "invalid perspective name" edge case specified for all tools that accept names, with consistent error behavior? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK039 - Is the "custom perspective with same name as built-in" edge case specified with requirements for how each tool handles the ambiguity? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK040 - Is the locale-dependent built-in perspective names edge case addressed with a concrete requirement (e.g., match by well-known identifier), or is it only noted as a concern? [Clarity, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK041 - Is the "concurrent perspective switches" edge case addressed with a requirement for how the system behaves, or only as a note that "last one wins"? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]

## Scenario Coverage -- UI Side-Effect Warning (switch_perspective)

- [ ] CHK042 - Does FR-022 specify that the tool description MUST include language indicating this is a "UI-affecting operation" or similar phrasing? [Completeness, Spec FR-022]
- [ ] CHK043 - Does the spec define whether the UI side-effect warning should advise AI assistants to confirm with the user before switching perspectives? [Clarity, Spec FR-022]
- [ ] CHK044 - Is the UI side-effect warning requirement testable -- can a contract test or acceptance scenario verify that the tool description contains the warning? [Measurability, Spec FR-022]
- [x] CHK045 - Does the spec address whether `switch_perspective` should return information about the PREVIOUS perspective (so the AI assistant can report what changed)? [Gap, Spec FR-021] -- RESOLVED: Updated FR-021 to include previous perspective name. Added `previousPerspective` field to SwitchPerspectiveSuccessSchema (nullable string) and SwitchResult entity in data-model.md.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality -- Measurability

- [ ] CHK046 - Can acceptance scenario US1-AS2 ("filter rule summary") be objectively verified -- is "summary" defined or is it the full `filterAggregation` value? [Measurability, Spec US1-AS2]
- [ ] CHK047 - Can acceptance scenario US2-AS1 ("full metadata") be objectively verified -- is the complete list of metadata fields enumerated? [Measurability, Spec US2-AS1]
- [ ] CHK048 - Can success criterion SC-001 ("<500ms for 50 custom perspectives") be objectively measured with the test infrastructure defined in the spec? [Measurability, Spec SC-001]
- [ ] CHK049 - Can success criterion SC-004 ("valid .ofocus-perspective file that can be re-imported") be objectively verified without manual OmniFocus interaction? [Measurability, Spec SC-004]
